definition: Non-GAAP FX reporting is systematically asymmetric, favoring management. Companies asymmetrically exclude unfavorable FX translation effects while retaining favorable transaction gains. Approximately 85% of multinationals with >30% international revenue report constant-currency metrics.
evidence: ~85% of multinationals with >30% international revenue report constant-currency metrics
Asymmetric exclusion of unfavorable FX translation while retaining favorable gains
Documented by SEC comment letters in 2015, 2018, and 2022
open_questions: Has SEC scrutiny reduced the asymmetry over time?
What is the quantitative impact on reported earnings growth rates?
Which sectors show the greatest asymmetry?
